coaching.title: Rest Day
coaching.message: Peak Phase, 66 days out — and today the work is rest. No run. The alpine fitness you're building gets locked in during recovery, not during the session. Your calendar has a gym slot and errands around Buenos Aires; keep any lifting light and non-taxing on the legs. Resist the urge to sneak in miles. A disciplined rest day inside a peak block is what lets next week's big vert sessions land.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Rest-day target ~220g carbs — don't slash them just because you're not running; glycogen replenishment is the whole point. Prioritize protein at ~1.8–2g/kg spread across meals for tissue repair, and lean on anti-inflammatory foods (oily fish, berries, olive oil, leafy greens). Buenos Aires makes protein easy — quality beef, eggs, plus fruit and rice for the carbs. Hydrate well; travel and dry air quietly dehydrate you.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Passive recovery only. A 20–30 min easy walk through Recoleta counts as active flush, not training. Add 10–15 min of mobility and foam rolling for hips, calves, and quads — the tissues that take a beating on alpine descents. Sleep is the headline: aim for 8+ hours tonight. If HRV is trending low from travel, that's your signal the rest day is landing exactly when needed.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Rest is a choice you make, not a session you skip. Peak weeks tempt you to chase volume — the mature athlete banks recovery instead. Picture the Julian Alps ridgelines: 2,500m of vert rewards the runner who arrives fresh, not the one who arrived overtrained. Trust the plan. Tomorrow you build; today you absorb.
